description: AICPA Service Organization Control 2 Type II — Trust Service Criteria for Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, and Privacy.

controls.title: Organisational Control Environment
controls.description: The organization demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values, and the board exercises oversight responsibility for the design and operation of the system of internal control.
controls.guidance: Document and communicate a code of conduct. Conduct annual ethics training. Board-level oversight of security controls via audit committee. Maintain documented governance structure.

controls.title: Logical and Physical Access Controls
controls.description: Restrict logical and physical access to the system to authorized individuals and protect against threats and unauthorized removal of data.
controls.guidance: Implement least-privilege access. Enforce MFA for all user accounts. Conduct quarterly access reviews. Remove access within 24 hours of termination. Use badge-based physical access controls.

controls.title: Credential and Authentication Management
controls.description: Manage credentials, authentication, and authorization throughout the user lifecycle.
controls.guidance: Enforce password complexity and rotation policies. Disable inactive accounts after 90 days. Use a centralized identity provider (IdP) with SSO. Audit authentication events.

controls.title: Risk Assessment Process
controls.description: Identify and assess risks to the achievement of objectives, including risks from changes in the environment.
controls.guidance: Conduct a formal risk assessment at least annually and upon significant changes. Document risk register with likelihood, impact, and risk owners. Track risk treatment plans to completion.

controls.title: Monitoring Controls
controls.description: Select, develop, and perform ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.
controls.guidance: Deploy continuous security monitoring tools (SIEM, IDS/IPS). Conduct quarterly internal control reviews. Respond to control deficiencies within defined SLAs. Report monitoring results to management.

controls.title: Control Activities — Change Management
controls.description: Select and develop control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
controls.guidance: Implement a formal change management process with approvals. Test changes in a staging environment before production deployment. Maintain a change log. Restrict production access to authorized change agents.

controls.title: Logical Access — System Boundaries
controls.description: Restrict access to the system to authorized users and protect against unauthorized access at the system boundary.
controls.guidance: Implement web application firewalls (WAF). Use network segmentation. Conduct penetration testing annually. Monitor and alert on unauthorized access attempts.

controls.title: Encryption Controls
controls.description: Protect sensitive data in transit and at rest using industry-standard encryption.
controls.guidance: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for all API and web traffic. Encrypt databases and storage volumes with AES-256. Manage encryption keys using a Hardware Security Module (HSM) or cloud KMS. Audit key usage.

controls.title: Security Incident Detection and Response
controls.description: Detect and respond to security incidents in a timely manner to minimize impact to the system and its users.
controls.guidance: Maintain a documented incident response plan. Test the plan through tabletop exercises twice per year. Define incident severity levels with response SLAs. Conduct post-incident reviews and capture lessons learned.

controls.title: Change Management — Secure Development
controls.description: Authorize, design, develop, acquire, implement, configure, and maintain infrastructure, data, software, and processes to support the achievement of the entity's objectives.
controls.guidance: Enforce code review for all changes to production systems. Use automated testing (unit, integration, SAST) in CI/CD pipelines. Conduct security design reviews for new features involving sensitive data.

controls.title: Risk Mitigation — Vendor and Third-Party Management
controls.description: Assess and manage risks introduced by vendors, business partners, and other third parties.
controls.guidance: Complete a security questionnaire for all vendors with access to customer data. Include security requirements in vendor contracts. Conduct annual vendor risk reviews. Monitor for vendor data breaches.

controls.title: System Availability Commitments
controls.description: The system is available for operation and use as committed or agreed upon in SLAs.
controls.guidance: Define and publish uptime SLAs (e.g., 99.9% monthly). Track availability with monitoring tools. Report uptime metrics to customers monthly. Maintain status pages with incident communications.

controls.title: Redundancy and Failover
controls.description: Implement redundant infrastructure to maintain service availability during component failures.
controls.guidance: Deploy across multiple availability zones or regions. Use load balancers and auto-scaling. Test failover procedures quarterly. Document and test disaster recovery plan (DRP).

controls.title: Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Maintain and test backups to ensure data can be recovered within defined recovery objectives.
controls.guidance: Perform daily automated backups. Encrypt backups at rest. Store backups in a separate geographic region. Test restoration monthly and document results. Define and meet RTO/RPO targets.

controls.title: Business Continuity Planning
controls.description: Develop, maintain, and test a business continuity plan (BCP) to ensure continued operations during disruptions.
controls.guidance: Review and update the BCP annually and after significant changes. Conduct BCP tests at least annually. Assign owners for all critical processes. Document escalation procedures.

controls.title: Data Processing Completeness and Accuracy
controls.description: System processing is complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.guidance: Implement input validation on all data entry points. Use checksums and reconciliation for batch processing. Log all processing errors and review daily. Conduct periodic data quality audits.

controls.title: Error Handling and Correction
controls.description: Detect and correct processing errors in a timely manner to maintain the integrity of data.
controls.guidance: Implement centralized error logging with alerting. Define SLAs for error resolution by severity. Notify customers of processing errors that affect their data. Document and remediate root causes.

controls.title: Confidential Information Identification and Classification
controls.description: Identify information designated as confidential and implement controls to protect it through its lifecycle.
controls.guidance: Implement a data classification policy with at least three tiers (Public, Internal, Confidential). Apply classification labels in documents and data systems. Train staff on handling each classification level.

controls.title: Confidential Information Disposal
controls.description: Dispose of confidential information in a manner consistent with the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
controls.guidance: Use certified secure deletion for digital files. Shred physical documents containing confidential information. Obtain destruction certificates from disposal vendors. Retain disposal records for audit purposes.

controls.title: Non-Disclosure Obligations
controls.description: Ensure employees, contractors, and vendors with access to confidential information are bound by non-disclosure agreements.
controls.guidance: Include NDAs in employment contracts and onboarding documentation. Require NDAs for all contractors and vendors with access to confidential data. Remind departing employees of ongoing NDA obligations.

controls.title: Privacy Notice and Communication
controls.description: Communicate to data subjects the purpose for which personal information is collected, used, retained, and disclosed.
controls.guidance: Publish a comprehensive and up-to-date privacy policy. Notify individuals at the point of data collection. Update the privacy policy within 30 days of any material change.

controls.title: Consent and Choice
controls.description: Obtain consent for collection, use, and disclosure of personal information and provide individuals with the ability to exercise choice.
controls.guidance: Use explicit opt-in consent for sensitive data. Provide opt-out mechanisms for marketing communications. Honor consent preferences within 10 business days.

controls.title: Collection of Personal Information
controls.description: Collect personal information only for the purposes identified in the privacy notice.
controls.guidance: Apply data minimization principles. Do not collect personal information beyond what is necessary. Document the legal basis for each data collection activity.

controls.title: Use, Retention, and Disposal of Personal Information
controls.description: Limit the use of personal information to the purposes for which it was collected, and retain it only as long as necessary.
controls.guidance: Implement automated data retention schedules. Conduct an annual data inventory and disposal exercise. Document retention periods for each data category.

controls.title: Access to Personal Information
controls.description: Provide individuals with access to their personal information and allow them to correct or update inaccurate information.
controls.guidance: Build a self-service privacy portal for data access and correction requests. Respond to requests within 30 days. Log all requests and outcomes.

controls.title: Disclosure and Notification
controls.description: Disclose personal information only to authorized parties and notify individuals of privacy breaches that may harm them.
controls.guidance: Maintain a record of all data disclosures. Notify affected individuals of privacy breaches within 72 hours. Notify regulators as required by applicable law.

controls.title: Quality of Personal Information
controls.description: Maintain accurate, complete, and relevant personal information.
controls.guidance: Allow users to update their information in the platform. Conduct periodic data quality reviews. Remove or correct inaccurate data upon request or discovery.

controls.title: Privacy Monitoring and Enforcement
controls.description: Monitor compliance with privacy policies and respond to privacy inquiries, complaints, and disputes.
controls.guidance: Designate a privacy officer or DPO. Publish a privacy contact mechanism. Track and resolve privacy complaints within 30 days. Conduct annual privacy compliance audits.
